item.title: Anthos On-Prem Storage
item.description: Overview Block/file storage is exposed to Anthos clusters via the Container Storage Interface (CSI) This is required for PersistentVolumes on Anthos on bare metal (and VMware—although the choice here might be easier) Google partners have developed CSI drivers that have been verified against Anthos: Dell EMC Hitachi HPE NetApp Portworx Pure Storage Robin.

item.title: GCP Load Balancers
item.description: Overview Type OSI Layer Options External Internal Locations HTTP(S) Layer 7 HTTP, HTTPS, HTTP/2 Yes Yes Single or multi-region TCP Layer 4 TCP, SSL Proxy, TCP Proxy Yes Yes Single or multi-region UDP Layer 4 UDP Yes Yes Single-region References

item.title: GCP AI ML Services
item.description: The AI/ML Lifecycle

item.title: GCP Big Data Services
item.description: Services Cloud IoT Core Pub/Sub BigQuery Dataprep Dataproc Google Cloud Dataflow Data Processing Decision Tree

item.title: GCP Compute Services
item.description: Services Overview Compute Engine Kubernetes Engine (GKE) App Engine Cloud Run Cloud Functions Choosing the Correct Service Autoscaling Service Min instances Min instances for HA How?

item.title: GCP ML Best Practices
item.description: Environment Setup Use Jupyter notebooks for development One instance per teammate per project Store prepared data and model in the same project Development Prepare a good amount of training data More accurate models Store tabular data in BigQuery Store unstructured data in Cloud Storage Processing Use TensorFlow Extended for TensorFlow projects Process tabular data BigQuery Process unstructured data with: Dataflow—General use cases, Apache Bean Dataproc—Apache Hadoop or Spark use cases Link data to model with managed datasets Production Specify hardware appropriate for model CPUs vs GPUs Plan for additional inputs to model Enable autoscaling References Google Vertex AI

item.title: Google App Engine
item.description: Overview PaaS serverless Regional No server management/config Comes in two flavours: App Engine Standard—first generation App Engine Flexible—second generation App Engine Standard Instantaneous scaling Scales to zero Runtimes: Python 3 Java 11 Node.

item.title: Google BigQuery
item.description: Overview Data warehouse Serverless Multi-regional SQL column store Terabytes to petabytes ML and BI Real-time analytics Automatic replication, 7 day storage of changes References GCP Big Data Services AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Dataflow
item.description: Overview Serverless—managed service, horizontal scaling Batch and streaming with a single model Often not the case Apache Beam Open source Beam SDK—Java, Python, Go (not currently supported) Dataflow is the backend for the Beam API References GCP Big Data Services Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Dataprep
item.description: Overview Explore, clean, prepare for analysis and ML Can detect schemas—data types, joins, anomalies Prediction of next transformation Transformation functions, e.

item.title: Google Cloud Dataproc
item.description: Overview Map/Reduce—Apache Spark and Hadoop Backed by Zonal VMs Can use preemptible VMs—reduce cost One VM must be non-preemptible References GCP Big Data Services Data Lifecycle AI ML Lifecycle

item.title: Google Cloud Functions
item.description: Overview FaaS Event driven Triggers: HTTP Cloud Storage Cloud Pub/Sub Cloud Firestore Audit logs Cloud Scheduler The glue between services—both in and outside GCP Good for streaming data and IoT References GCP Compute Services

item.title: Google Cloud IoT Core
item.description: Overview Fully managed—connect, manage, ingest device data Global Device Manager Identity Authentication Configuration Control Protocol Bridge Incoming telemetry data to Pub/Sub HTTPS or MQTT Two-way comms Updates on and offline References = GCP Big Data Services

item.title: Google Cloud Pub/Sub
item.description: Overview Global messaging—consistent latency globally Scalable, durable Topic-based At-least-once model Push and pull modes Exactly once processing Messages can be ordered and filtered References GCP Big Data Services

item.title: Google Cloud Run
item.description: Overview Regional Fully managed PaaS Containerized applications Knative-based Any language/library/binary Autoscaling from zero References GCP Compute Services

item.title: Google Cloud Storage
item.description: References

item.title: Google Compute Engine
item.description: Overview Highly configurable Zonal Choice of machine types: General purpose Compute optimized Memory optimized Processor optimized (GPUs) Public or private boot disk images Preemptible VMs Can stop at any time Will definitely stop after 24 hrs 60 – 90% cheaper than standard VMs Sole Tenant Nodes Dedicated server Useful for specific hardware requirements, e.

item.title: Google Kubernetes Engine
item.description: Overview Two modes: Standard—total control Autopilot—fully managed Auto-repair and upgrade Autoscaling References GCP Compute Services Kubernetes

item.title: Vertex AI
item.description: Vision API Vision detection Labelling Face / landmark recognition OCR Explicit content tagging Video Intelligence API Annotate videos From GCS, live streams or local videos Contextual info From single frame to entire video Recognise text Track objects Recognise people Translation API 100+ language translation pairs Language detection Basic or advanced versions Advanced—batch requests, Auto ML custom models Text-to-Speech and Speech-to-Text APIs Fast text-to-speech and vice versa Natural Language API Text analysis—insights from documents (e.

item.title: Mountkirk Games Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: GCP Compute Engine Machine Families
item.description: Use Cases Family Use case General purpose (E2/N2/N2D/N1) Balanced performance and cost Compute optimized (C2/C2D) High-performance, CPU frequency and consistency, powerful cores, high CPU/mem ratios Memory optimized (M1/M2) High memory requirements Accelerator optimized (A2) Highest performance GPUs for ML, HPC and parallel computing Scale-out optimized (Tau T2D) Horizontally scalable cloud-native workloads GPUs GPUs supported on: N1 A2

item.title: Helicopter Racing League Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: Anthos
item.description: Components GKE Multi Cluster Ingress References Kubernetes

item.title: Multi Cluster Ingress
item.description: Overview Google hosted service to load balance requests across GKE clusters and regions. The MultiClusterIngress Kubernetes resource is a controller for External HTTP(S) Load Balancers.

item.title: NEGs
item.description: Network Endpoint Groups To be completed… References

item.title: Compliance
item.description: Key Compliance Areas Legislation US healthcare: HIPAA Need Business Associate Addendum from GCP Commercial, e.

item.title: EHR Healthcare Case Study
item.description: Non-italicised points represent notes taken from Google’s official case study documentation, with keywords highlighted in bold. Points in italics represent additional insights and possible solutions.

item.title: GCP Annotations
item.description: Security Marks For compliance purposes Adds business context to assets Adds additional layer of security Applied in the Cloud Security Command Center (CSCC) CSCC also works with labels and network tags Organization, project, or resource level Labels Key-value pairs Use cases: Attribute resources to teams or cost-centres Identify environments Cost/billing allocation Group resources to monitoring and analytics by operations Can apply to projects, but not folders Network Tags Applied to VM instances Used in firewall rules and network routes Flow Chart References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect

item.title: GKE Cluster Autoscaling
item.description: Scaling Down Clusters can be prevented from scaling down, and deleting nodes due to: Pod affinity/anti-affinity rules preventing them from being evicted Pods with local storage Pods not managed by a controller, such as a Deployment, StatefulSet etc.

item.title: GCP Data Lifecycle
item.description: Ingest Stream Cloud Pub/Sub Batch Cloud Storage Storage Transfer Service—moving large amounts of data from on-prem BigQuery BigQuery Transfer Service—moving from SaaS e.

item.title: GCP PCA Exam Guide
item.description: PCA’s need to: Design and plan cloud solution architectures Business focussed Understand technical requirements Networking, storage, compute Migration planning Plan for future improvements Manage and configure solution infrastructure Network topologies Data storage Compute—data processing Design for security and compliance Analysing and optimizing technical and business processes Managing implementation References Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect] Professional Cloud Architect Certification Exam Guide

item.title: Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect
item.description: Key Fundamentals To Know Distributed systems OSI Layers TCP/IP How it works IPv4 and IPV6 Public vs private RFC1918 addressing SSL/TLS SSH Common TCP Ports HTTP/S URLs and URIs Verbs Caching CORS HTTP Status Codes Site Reliability Engineering SLIs, SLOs and SLAs Case Studies EHR Healthcare Helicopter Racing League Mountkirk Games TerramEarth
